actionable_feedback: {'item': 'Add a dedicated Screening & KYC sheet to the workbook.', 'rationale': 'The required analysis references screening hits and KYC facts; an explicit tab improves auditability and rubric alignment.', 'acceptance_criteria': ["Workbook contains a sheet named exactly 'Screening & KYC'.", 'Sheet summarizes: customer/UBO details, document status (e.g., beneficial-owner passport expiry 2025-12-01), screening hits with IDs and scores (S1=86, S2=72, P1=64), onboarding basics (expected activity, jurisdictions), and policy thresholds for actions.', 'Include a small cross-reference legend mapping synonymous tabs (e.g., Dashboard  Case Summary).']}

{'item': 'Clarify sanctions-policy-consistent actions for 70-84 near matches.', 'rationale': "Volga Transit DMCC (score 72) was described as under 'manual review hold', which can be read as a sanctions-triggered hold outside the >=85 policy threshold.", 'acceptance_criteria': ["Dashboard/Case Summary and Sanctions Queue specify: '>=85 potential match: place on sanctions hold pending escalation; 70-84: route to Sanctions Escalation Queue without automatic sanctions hold.'", 'If an operational hold is recommended for Volga Transit DMCC, explicitly cite the non-sanctions basis (e.g., TBML/document anomalies such as reused container ID MSKU-773102 and blank vessel) and reference the applicable packet rules.']}

{'item': 'Make the overvaluation ratio calculation visibly reproducible.', 'rationale': 'Grader referenced the ratio; showing the formula improves reproducibility.', 'acceptance_criteria': ['Risk Scoring (or equivalent) displays payment amount, comparable customs value, and a formula cell computing the ratio (e.g., =Payment/ComparableValue) with the 5.28x result for T1.', "Add a short note indicating the rule trigger (e.g., '>3x')."]}

{'item': 'Add a graph legend/adjacency list for the Transaction Graph.', 'rationale': 'Facilitates quick reproduction and cross-checking of nodes and flows.', 'acceptance_criteria': ['Transaction Graph (or a companion section) includes a table with: Node From, Node To, Txn IDs (T1-T7), dates, amounts, and any flags (e.g., bank change in T2, split wires in T6).']}

{'item': 'Tighten Evidence Table traceability.', 'rationale': 'Enhances audit-readiness for spot checks.', 'acceptance_criteria': ['Each row references packet IDs and specific fields/documents (e.g., INV-441, HS 8542.31, MSKU-773102, wallet W-9A, T2 bank change), and, where applicable, workbook cell references.']}

{'item': "Add a concise 'Methods' and 'Limitations' note.", 'rationale': 'Clarifies how metrics were computed and the packet-only scope.', 'acceptance_criteria': ["Workbook includes a short 'Methods' section explaining how totals and ratios (e.g., SUM of rule points) are computed.", "Narrative or Dashboard includes a 'Limitations' note affirming packet-only assessment and potential-match handling."]}

{'item': 'Sanctions Queue columns to reflect policy thresholds and ownership.', 'rationale': 'Operational clarity on who does what and when.', 'acceptance_criteria': ['Sanctions Queue adds columns: match_score, threshold_action (>=85 hold; 70-84 escalate, no automatic hold), escalation_owner, and hold_required (Yes/No with footnote for TBML-based operational holds).']}
artifact_specific_comments: {'artifact': 'attempts/baseline/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT]', 'comment': "Six key sheets present; add 'Screening & KYC' and a graph legend/adjacency list. Ensure Risk Scoring shows the overvaluation formula and Sanctions Queue reflects threshold actions."}

{'artifact': 'attempts/baseline/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT]', 'comment': 'Narrative is packet-grounded and uses potential-match language. Refine the Volga Transit DMCC recommendation to avoid implying a sanctions-triggered hold at score 72 unless TBML/document anomalies are explicitly cited as the independent hold basis.'}
feedback_summary: Strong deliverable coverage and packet-grounded narrative. Two notable improvement areas: (1) workbook lacks an explicitly labeled Screening & KYC tab; and (2) sanctions policy alignment needs clearer distinction between >=85 holds versus 70-84 near matches (Volga Transit DMCC at 72) to avoid implying an automatic hold unless independently justified by TBML rules.

grader_observations: Workbook sheets registered: Dashboard, Transactions, Risk Scoring, Sanctions Queue, Evidence Table, Transaction Graph.
Workbook metadata shows a total case score of 156 and a SUM formula.
Narrative references T1-T7, S1-S2, P1 and key fields (HS 8542.31, MSKU-773102, INV-441).
verifier_notes: Minor scoring-metadata inconsistency in grader output does not affect deliverables.

metadata_json.public_response_summary: Model evaluator generated actionable feedback and revision plan.

acceptance_criteria: Workbook contains a sheet named exactly 'Screening & KYC'.

Sheet summarizes: customer/UBO details, document status (e.g., beneficial-owner passport expiry 2025-12-01), screening hits with IDs and scores (S1=86, S2=72, P1=64), onboarding basics (expected activity, jurisdictions), and policy thresholds for actions.
Include a small cross-reference legend mapping synonymous tabs (e.g., Dashboard  Case Summary).
actionable_feedback.item: Add a dedicated Screening & KYC sheet to the workbook.
actionable_feedback.rationale: The required analysis references screening hits and KYC facts; an explicit tab improves auditability and rubric alignment.

acceptance_criteria: Dashboard/Case Summary and Sanctions Queue specify: '>=85 potential match: place on sanctions hold pending escalation; 70-84: route to Sanctions Escalation Queue without automatic sanctions hold.'

If an operational hold is recommended for Volga Transit DMCC, explicitly cite the non-sanctions basis (e.g., TBML/document anomalies such as reused container ID MSKU-773102 and blank vessel) and reference the applicable packet rules.
actionable_feedback.item: Clarify sanctions-policy-consistent actions for 70-84 near matches.
actionable_feedback.rationale: Volga Transit DMCC (score 72) was described as under 'manual review hold', which can be read as a sanctions-triggered hold outside the >=85 policy threshold.

acceptance_criteria: Risk Scoring (or equivalent) displays payment amount, comparable customs value, and a formula cell computing the ratio (e.g., =Payment/ComparableValue) with the 5.28x result for T1.
Add a short note indicating the rule trigger (e.g., '>3x').
actionable_feedback.item: Make the overvaluation ratio calculation visibly reproducible.
actionable_feedback.rationale: Grader referenced the ratio; showing the formula improves reproducibility.

acceptance_criteria: Transaction Graph (or a companion section) includes a table with: Node From, Node To, Txn IDs (T1-T7), dates, amounts, and any flags (e.g., bank change in T2, split wires in T6).
actionable_feedback.item: Add a graph legend/adjacency list for the Transaction Graph.
actionable_feedback.rationale: Facilitates quick reproduction and cross-checking of nodes and flows.

acceptance_criteria: Each row references packet IDs and specific fields/documents (e.g., INV-441, HS 8542.31, MSKU-773102, wallet W-9A, T2 bank change), and, where applicable, workbook cell references.
Workbook includes a short 'Methods' section explaining how totals and ratios (e.g., SUM of rule points) are computed.
Narrative or Dashboard includes a 'Limitations' note affirming packet-only assessment and potential-match handling.
actionable_feedback.rationale: Clarifies how metrics were computed and the packet-only scope.

acceptance_criteria: Sanctions Queue adds columns: match_score, threshold_action (>=85 hold; 70-84 escalate, no automatic hold), escalation_owner, and hold_required (Yes/No with footnote for TBML-based operational holds).
actionable_feedback.item: Sanctions Queue columns to reflect policy thresholds and ownership.
actionable_feedback.rationale: Operational clarity on who does what and when.

artifact_specific_comments.comment: Six key sheets present; add 'Screening & KYC' and a graph legend/adjacency list. Ensure Risk Scoring shows the overvaluation formula and Sanctions Queue reflects threshold actions.

Narrative is packet-grounded and uses potential-match language. Refine the Volga Transit DMCC recommendation to avoid implying a sanctions-triggered hold at score 72 unless TBML/document anomalies are explicitly cited as the independent hold basis.

raw_llm_output.feedback_summary: Strong deliverable coverage and packet-grounded narrative. Two notable improvement areas: (1) workbook lacks an explicitly labeled Screening & KYC tab; and (2) sanctions policy alignment needs clearer distinction between >=85 holds versus 70-84 near matches (Volga Transit DMCC at 72) to avoid implying an automatic hold unless independently justified by TBML rules.

Narrative references T1-T7, S1-S2, P1 and key fields (HS 8542.31, MSKU-773102, INV-441).

raw_llm_output.suggested_revision: Update the workbook to include a dedicated Screening & KYC tab, formalize sanctions actions to differentiate >=85 holds from 70-84 escalations without automatic holds, and explicitly justify any Volga Transit DMCC operational hold via TBML/document anomalies. Add visible overvaluation ratio formulas, a transaction graph legend/adjacency list, stronger evidence traceability with packet/field references, and brief Methods/Limitations notes. Adjust the narrative's Recommended Actions to mirror the clarified policy and TBML rationale.
